title: Temple of Hathor
description: The remarkably preserved main temple of the Dendera Complex, featuring the famous zodiac ceiling and crypts. Ptolemaic/Roman Era.

title: Roman Mammisi of Dendera
description: Birth house dedicated to the divine birth of Ihy, built by Trajan and Marcus Aurelius. Dendera Complex.

title: Mammisi of Nectanebo II
description: The older birth house at Dendera, dating to the 30th Dynasty. Dendera Complex.

title: Temple of Isis at Dendera
description: A small temple located south of the main Hathor temple, built by Augustus. Dendera Complex.

title: Sacred Lake of Dendera
description: A rectangular, stone-lined pool used for ritual purification by priests. Dendera Complex.

title: Coptic Basilica of Dendera
description: Ruins of a 5th-century Christian basilica built within the temple precincts. Dendera Complex.

title: Dendera Sanatorium
description: Mudbrick structure used for therapeutic incubation and healing rituals. Dendera Complex.

title: Gate of Domitian and Trajan
description: The monumental northern gateway to the Dendera temple enclosure.

title: Dendera Necropolis
description: Ancient cemetery containing mastabas from the Old Kingdom to the First Intermediate Period.

title: Temple of Min Ruins
description: Remains of the temple dedicated to Min, the fertility god, in Coptos (Qift). Ptolemaic/Roman Era.

title: Temple of Isis at Coptos
description: Ruins of the southern temple in Coptos dedicated to Isis and Geb. Qift.

title: El-Qala Temple
description: A small, well-preserved Roman temple dedicated to Claudius, located north of Coptos.

title: Rein El-Qift
description: The massive mudbrick enclosure walls and fortress remains of ancient Coptos.

title: Wadi Hammamat Inscriptions
description: Ancient quarrying site with hundreds of hieroglyphic rock inscriptions. Qift-Quseir Road.

title: Bir Fawakhir
description: Byzantine gold mining settlement and wells on the route to the Red Sea. Eastern Desert.

title: Temple of Set (Ombos)
description: Ruins of the New Kingdom temple dedicated to the god Set in Nubt (Tukh). Naqada Region.

title: Step Pyramid of Ombos
description: A small step pyramid dating to the late 3rd Dynasty (likely Huni or Sneferu) in Tukh. Naqada Region.

title: South Town (Nubt)
description: One of the most important Predynastic settlements in Egypt (Zawayda). Naqada Region.

title: Naqada Predynastic Necropolis
description: The type-site for the Naqada culture, containing thousands of prehistoric graves.

title: Monastery of St. George (Naqada)
description: Deir Mari Girgis, a large Coptic monastic complex on the west bank. Naqada.

title: Monastery of the Archangel Michael
description: Deir el-Malak, a historic monastery with ancient churches. Naqada Desert.

title: Monastery of the Holy Cross
description: Deir el-Salib, known for its unique architectural style. Naqada West Bank.

title: Monastery of St. Victor
description: Deir Mari Boqtor, located in the solitude of the desert. Naqada West Bank.

title: Temple of Haroeris (Ptolemaic Gate)
description: Ruins of the Ptolemaic temple of Haroeris and Heqet, famously visited by Napoleon's savants. Qus.

title: Al-Amri Mosque
description: Founded in the 10th century, containing elements from the Fatimid and Mamluk eras. Oldest in Qus.

title: Shrine of Sheikh Al-Qusawi
description: Mausoleum of the local Sufi saint, a spiritual center in Qus.

title: Coptic Church of St. Stephen
description: A historic church in Qus dating back to the 18th century with notable icons.

title: Temple of Shenhur
description: Temple of Isis built by Augustus and Trajan, located between Qus and Luxor.

title: Palace of Prince Youssef Kamal
description: An architectural jewel blending Islamic, European, and Mamluk styles (early 20th century). Nag Hammadi.

title: Jabal al-Tarif
description: The mountain cliffs near where the Gnostic Gospels (Nag Hammadi Library) were discovered.

title: Chenoboskion (Al-Qasr)
description: Ancient city and site of one of the earliest Pachomian monasteries. Near Nag Hammadi.

title: Monastery of Anba Balamun
description: An ancient Coptic monastery located in Qasr el-Sayyad. Dating to early Christian era.

title: Tombs of Qasr el-Sayyad
description: Rock-cut tombs of Old Kingdom nomarchs (6th Dynasty) overlooking the Nile.

title: Temple of Diospolis Parva (Hou)
description: Fragmentary remains of a Ptolemaic/Roman temple in the ancient city of Hiw.

title: Deir el-Ballas North Palace
description: Royal residence of the Theban 17th Dynasty kings (Seqenenre Tao). North of Thebes.

title: Deir el-Ballas South Palace
description: A massive mudbrick platform and settlement area acting as a military staging post for the expulsion of the Hyksos.

title: Mosque of Sidi Abdel Rahim El-Qenawi
description: The most significant mosque in Qena, housing the tomb of the famous Sufi saint (12th century).

title: Sabil of Ali Bey Mubarak
description: A historic Ottoman-era public water dispensary (Sabil) in Qena City.

title: Qena Governorate Museum
description: Museum housing artifacts from the region, from Prehistoric to Islamic eras.

title: Al-Ansari Mosque
description: Historic mosque in Farshut, notable for its minaret and local religious importance.

title: Wadi el-Höl Inscriptions
description: Site of early alphabetic inscriptions found on the Farshut Road in the desert west of Qena.

title: Nag Hammadi Barrage
description: Historic irrigation dam built in the early 20th century, a marvel of modern engineering of its time.

title: Monastery of Saint Pisentius
description: Deir Anba Bisada, located on the west bank near Naqada, dedicated to the Bishop of Coptos.

title: Chapel of Hathor (Wadi Hammamat)
description: A rock-cut paneion or shrine dedicated to Hathor/Isis by quarry expeditions. Eastern Desert.

title: Ezbet el-Bousa Site
description: Archaeological site near Dishna featuring Predynastic remains.

title: El-Qasar Basilica Ruins
description: Remains of a large basilica in the ancient city of Chenoboskion.

title: Mosque of Al-Omari (Dendera)
description: An ancient mosque located in the village of Dendera, distinct from the one in Qus.
